name: Rich Text Editor
summary: The rich-text editing Block of the Web Editing standard (#618). Resolves the swappable CustomEditorEngineRegistry (editor-engine protocol, #629) — native-first contenteditable+InputEvent by default, with ProseMirror/Lexical/Slate/Quill registered as alternative engine impls behind the same interface. Implements the rich-text intent and composes the text-formatting intent (#630) for the formatting surface; composes the sanitizer plug (CustomSanitizerRegistry, #631) on paste so external HTML is normalized before it enters the document model. Decorations (active-format ranges, spellcheck/find marks) paint via the CSS Custom Highlight API by default — no <span> soup in the editable region.

contentEditable.pattern: contenteditable + the InputEvent API (beforeinput/input, inputType) as the native-first editing surface
contentEditable.adoption: The default engine drives a contenteditable region through beforeinput, reading inputType to map native editing intents to document-model operations — no library required for the floor.

editContext.pattern: EditContext API — decoupled editing surface for IME/selection without contenteditable quirks
editContext.adoption: Where available, the engine upgrades from the contenteditable floor to EditContext for robust IME composition; the negotiation is the editing-surface capability tier (#628).

cssCustomHighlight.pattern: CSS Custom Highlight API for editor decorations (active format, find, spellcheck ranges)
cssCustomHighlight.adoption: Decorations register as Highlights painted via ::highlight(), so the editable text node stays clean and decorations never corrupt the document model.

engineIsSwappable.description: The editor resolves CustomEditorEngineRegistry; the native contenteditable+InputEvent engine is the default
engineIsSwappable.rationale: editor-engine protocol (#629): swapping the native default for a ProseMirror/Lexical adapter leaves the document model untouched because the engine reads/writes the structured pivot, never a vendor doc format — the spec is the only lock.

sanitizeOnPaste.description: Pasted external HTML passes through the CustomSanitizerRegistry plug before entering the model
sanitizeOnPaste.rationale: Untrusted paste is the primary injection vector for a rich-text surface; routing it through the shared sanitizer plug (#631) keeps the policy swappable and consistent, not re-implemented per editor.

formattingIsAComposedIntent.description: The toolbar/formatting UX is the composed text-formatting intent (#630), not owned by this block
formattingIsAComposedIntent.rationale: Separation of concerns: the editor owns the editing surface + engine seam; the formatting affordances are a distinct, reusable intent the block composes.

nativeFirstSurface.description: contenteditable floor, EditContext upgrade — library engines are opt-in
nativeFirstSurface.rationale: Native-first default per #628 substrate negotiation: the platform editing surface is the baseline, with library engines as registered enhancements rather than the floor.
